skill.name: explain-expert
skill.description: Analyze a codebase and produce a structured technical overview of its components, interactions, deployment setup, and runtime behavior.
skill.summary: Create structured architecture explanations for unfamiliar codebases.
skill.author: nexteacc

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported weak cryptography and system reconnaissance indicators, but review found only documentation text and a prior evaluation JSON artifact. No executable code, network access, credential handling, file writes, external commands, or prompt injection attempts were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: Static weak cryptography alerts were triggered by ordinary words in documentation and evaluation text. The cited files contain no cryptographic functions, hash algorithms, encryption settings, or executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are descriptive prose about architecture analysis and documentation. I found no code path, algorithm use, or security-sensitive cryptographic behavior in those locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Wording
low_findings.description: Static system reconnaissance alerts map to instructions for understanding a user-provided codebase. The skill asks the assistant to explain project structure and runtime behavior, not to probe the host system or collect environment data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited wording is ordinary guidance for codebase explanation. There is no shell command, filesystem traversal implementation, host enumeration logic, or data exfiltration channel.

content.value_statement: Unfamiliar repositories are difficult to understand quickly. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through a structured architecture review.
actual_capabilities: Identifies main directories, configuration files, and application entry points.
Maps core components and explains their responsibilities.
Describes component interactions, APIs, data flow, and control flow.
Summarizes build steps, deployment setup, environments, and infrastructure clues.
Explains application startup, request handling, workflows, errors, and background tasks.
Produces developer-focused technical overview material for documentation.
limitations: It depends on repository files available in the current workspace.
It does not run tests, execute code, or verify runtime behavior directly.
It does not modify files unless the user separately requests edits.
It may miss behavior hidden in generated files or unavailable services.

use_cases.title: Understand a new repository
use_cases.description: Get a structured explanation of project layout, entry points, major modules, and runtime flow.

use_cases.title: Prepare architecture context
use_cases.description: Create a concise technical overview before planning migrations, reviews, or team handoffs.

use_cases.title: Draft system documentation
use_cases.description: Turn repository structure and implementation details into clear documentation starter material.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Codebase Overview
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner scan of an unfamiliar project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain this codebase. Identify the main directories, entry points, core components, and overall purpose.

prompt_templates.title: Component Interaction Map
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze how the major components interact. Include data flow, control flow, APIs, and important dependencies.

prompt_templates.title: Deployment and Runtime Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review for operations or handoff.
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain the deployment architecture and runtime behavior. Cover build steps, environments, startup flow, request handling, and background work.

prompt_templates.title: Full Technical Documentation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced documentation draft for a team.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete technical overview of this repository. Cover structure, dependencies, components, interactions, deployment, runtime behavior, and key assumptions.

output_examples.input: Explain this codebase for a new developer joining the team.
output: Starts with the repository layout and primary entry points.
Explains how requests or jobs move through the system.
Highlights configuration files that affect deployment.

output_examples.input: Document the deployment and runtime behavior of this service.
output: Lists required environment assumptions visible in configuration.
Explains startup order and request handling.
Notes background tasks and error handling paths when visible.

best_practices: Provide repository access and ask for a structured overview before deep questions.
Request separate sections for structure, components, interactions, deployment, and runtime behavior.
Ask the assistant to cite important files so reviewers can verify claims.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for broad architecture claims without allowing file inspection.
Do not treat the overview as runtime proof without tests or logs.
Do not request implementation changes in the same prompt as neutral explanation.

faq.question: What does this skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces a structured technical overview of a codebase and how its main parts work together.

faq.question: Does it edit my repository?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed for explanation and documentation, not file modification.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. It is listed as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it execute project code?
faq.answer: No. It describes what can be inferred from files unless the user asks for separate execution.

faq.question: What files are most useful?
faq.answer: README files, package manifests, dependency files, Docker files, configuration files, and main source entry points are useful.

faq.question: Is this a security review skill?
faq.answer: No. It can explain architecture, but it does not replace a focused security audit.
